section: Open knobs / decisions to surface (not silently chosen)
anchor: Which dataset to SFT on: the full balanced Plan-A run (≥150/≥150) vs a smoke JSO
text:
okay, cool, u can do all the stuff in the plan, but since I am going to sleep, i want u to try to execute the full training experiment. here is what I want. 

we are going to train qwen3-4B and Qwen3-4B-I as the judge. For now train on all the constraints, maybe do eval on 400 samples, try to find good and bad samples, do the dataset generation, then do the SFT. u can try a few lr and configs to see which one is the best. u can do full FT. I gave u 2 GPUs u can use already, right? u can scancel them once ur done using them. 

after u did the training (which does not depend on the regular LLM monitor judge which determines whether the user followed the task -- we don't even need to run it here), I want to do an evaluation (can be on a held out set which u can generate as follows -- if u tell the eval to do random 500 samples, then u can train on the first 400, and then eval on the rest -- might need to add some start_index buisness to the build_dataset function of ift). when we do the evaluation, we should use the regular LLM judge, and like we do in confessions (look at _m_confessions where we have a postfix that says "we asked for a confession and here's what the model said! -- I want to do the same for self incrimination. Something like -- note that when the model makes the tool call " ... " then it thinks that it did not follow the user's instructions. 

any questions?

thread.text: Got it — executing the full experiment autonomously overnight. Plan: (1) serve Qwen3-4B (actor, GPU0:8000) + Qwen3-4B-I (confidence judge, GPU1:8001) via vLLM; (2) add start_index to ift build_dataset for a disjoint train[0:400]/heldout[400:500] split over ALL constraints+difficulties; (3) generate the self-incrimination dataset (rule-based correctness + Qwen3-4B-I hedging judge, NO regular monitor) over the 400 train tasks, label+append report_scheming(); (4) export to SFT messages dataset; (5) FULL fine-tune Qwen3-4B on 2 GPUs, sweeping a few LRs; (6) held-out eval on the 100: serve the SFT'd model, run the REGULAR LLM monitor judge in two variants — baseline vs a report_scheming()-aware postfix (mirroring _m_confessions MONITOR_CONFESSION_PREFACE) — and measure whether the self-report improves the monitor's detection of task-failure. Will scancel the GPUs when done. Assumption I'm running with: the actor that generates the training rollouts = Qwen3-4B itself (self-incrimination on its own behavior). Writing an experiment plan + tweak log to claude_state; will leave a full report for the morning.
